title: Level 218
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Level 218: "Blizzard Warning"'''}} {{Glacier Theme}}
You are viewing an outdated file; some information may be inaccurate or a placeholder.
{{Class Custom Image|class=4|hexagon= 4|safety=Unsafe|security=Notable Environmental Dangers|entity=Presence of Singular Entity|bg-image=Bluebackground159.png|text-color=#ffffff}} ---- {{Blurimage|file=Level -21 background.jpg |caption=One of the earliest known images of Level 218.}} '''Level 218''' is the 219th Level of the Backrooms. == Description == '''Level 218''' is a vast outdoor area of undetermined sizeAlthough it is confirmed to be finite. filled almost exclusively with streets and large residential buildings. The streets are relatively barren, sparsely containing various streetlamps, signs, wire fences, scattered cars, and various other mundane objects. Light snow is constantly falling on '''Level 218''', rendering navigation moderately difficult without proper equipment and protection. It is completely blanketed in snow ranging from 3 to 75 centimeters deep. Snow exceeding this threshold will cause the bottom layer to [[The Void|no-clip]] to an unknown location, alongside anything surrounded by it. No [[Object List|objects]] or supplies can be found here. This area of '''Level 218''' sits at a constant temperature of -1° Celsius (30° Fahrenheit), forcing most Wanderers to either wear heavy clothes or remain indoors. Inside the buildings of '''Level 218''' exists the interior zone. Some consider this section a separate sublevel. They resemble networks of apartment hallways that lack purpose or destination. It possesses white walls, varying floor patterns, and a drop ceiling. The interior space of '''Level 218''' maintains a temperature of 21° Celsius (70° Fahrenheit). This area of '''Level 218''' is highly non-Euclidean and often only occupies a meager amount of what the building's interior seems to be. Notably, they may run between unconnected buildings allowing for gigantic complexes to connect potentially hundreds of buildings.The largest is estimated at about 70 kilometers2. The complex lacks rooms, with doors often connecting two hallways. Wanderers may come across occasional windows that showcase the outside world although they may also be [[Entity 2|their predatory counterpart]]. These windows line up with windows in the exterior area and appear lit up on the sides of buildings. This area is rich in naturally occurring objects making it quite desirable for settlements. {{Blurimage|file=Green Hotel Hallway.webp|caption=An image of the interior zone.}} '''Level 218''' has frequent blizzards which can devastate large areas. These storms appear to follow a certain pattern, with large storms being less dangerous and smaller storms being extremely dangerous. There is a theory that the entire level may be inside a giant blizzard, causing the constant snowfall. Wind speeds in some rarely occurring storms can get so high that even the interior areas of '''Level 218''' can be damaged. Snowflakes themselves also possess a unique property here, as they are incapable of melting, even outside the level.But considering they are otherwise benign when removed, an object classification is unnessescary The sky stuck in a constant state of nighttime and lacks clouds and celestial bodies. '''Level 218''' has a stable Wi-Fi connection. ==Entities== '''Level 218''' is completely devoid of [[Phenomenon 10|spawning entities]] excluding the afforementioned [[Entity 2|Windows]]. Very few instances of infectious entities (such as those affected by [[Phenomenon 35|Roth Syndrome]]) exist, as the few found here are actively hunted by resident groups.
As of April 8th, 2015, the [[M.E.G.]] has confirmed the existence and hostility of a singular, unique entity inside '''Level 218'''. Designated "The Orb," this entity manifests as a glowing sphere believed to be ~2 meters in diameter and capable of flight at speeds of 20 kilometers an hour. The presence of this entity causes an intense blizzard capable of causing catastrophic damage to the structures of '''Level 218'''. A temporary reclassification to Level 4 and evacuation efforts have begun until an appropriate team neutralizes the entity. Efforts are still ongoing. ==Colonies and Outposts== ===M.E.G. Base Bonfire === *Houses about 50 members (20 M.E.G. operatives and 30 Wanderers). *Almost entirely within the interior zone. *Operates as an engineering facility. *Known for being slightly more cooperative than most of M.E.G. *Open to new wanderers but not trade. *Guards an exit to [[Level 20]]. ===Crystal Township=== *Made up of 17 permanent members. *Mostly within the interior zone, but contains some exterior territory. *Purely residential, with little in the way of defense. * Under M.E.G. protection. *Open to trade and temporary residents. ==Entrances and Exits== === Entrances=== *On [[Level 80]], one may come across a door similar to the ones that lead to the hallway, but with a "218" crudely written above it. Entering this door will lead you into the interior area. *On [[Level 74]], one can no-clip through a streetlamp and end up in the exterior. *On [[Level 118]], one can walk down a staircase in the ground to arrive in the exterior. *On [[Level 523]], large residential buildings may trigger a forced no-clip into either area of '''Level 218'''. ===Exits === *No-clipping into deep snow piles sometimes leads to [[Level 306]]. Success is guaranteed if the destination one will end up in the level is snowing. *Passing from the interior area to the exterior will in rare cases send one to [[Level 107]] instead of outside. *Going into a building that resembles a warehouse will take one to [[Level 20]].
Addendum 1:
[[Vouchsafed Contrivance|████]] Mission Summary:
*Captain: Maizie Frost *Vice-Captain: Sebastian Hayes *Combatant(s): Michael Sparks, Lucielle Thompson, Zachary Grey *Objective: Terminate the Orb *Date: April 22nd, 2015 *Source: Body Camera footage of Michael Sparks, radio logs ===Begin Excerpt=== ---- [''The footage starts showing what appears to be a road in the exterior of '''Level 218'''. THOMPSON can be seen facing the camera and FROST is walking towards the camera.'']
SPARKS: I can't believe we're already prepared to kill the thing.
THOMPSON: With all the work Maizie is having us do, I'm surprised we didn't have it done in a day.
[''SPARKS can be heard chuckling as FROST enters the focus before he quickly goes quiet.'']
FROST: Now is not the time to be goofing off. We've got work to do.
THOMSPON: We always have work to do. And we're about to finish a mission! Isn't there room for a bit of happiness for once?
SPARKS: Lucy, I don't think it's best to-
FROST: You know by now that we celebrate, ''after'' the mission is over. Anyhow, Hayes is finishing up with the [[Greek Fire|Liquid Inferno]] payload. We'll be ready to start in several minutes. Don't get yourself killed Thompson.
THOMPSON: I do have the most important role don't I? I'd rather not end up blowing myself up.
SPARKS: You'll do fine Lucy.
====- 30 minutes of footage omitted for brevity -====
[''The camera now shows a plain of snow with several large buildings in the distance. FROST can be seen holding a rifle. HAYES and GREY are at a workstation in a tent.'']
HAYES: Lucielle, are you in position?
THOMPSON: Affirmative, the payload has been planted. I can feel that thing whipping up some snow.
GREY: It's only half a kilometer out I believe. Don't move just yet.
SPARKS: She's in that thing's path! You're not getting her killed.
[''FROST'' is heard sighing, HAYES walks out of the tent and faces away from the camera'']
'' THOMPSON: Mikey, I'll be fine!
FROST: Sparks, you don't normally behave like this...
GREY: I don't think any of us are in the right mind, it has been non-stop work, worth it or not.
SPARKS: Sorry Captain! And thank you, Grey.
HAYES: Romantic relationships are bad for missions like this Michael.
[''SPARKS lets out a choked gasp. There is silence for a few moments before FROST turns to face the camera and runs closer.'']
FROST: Sparks! You know relationships aren't allowed! This is unacceptable behavior! Especially from you of all people.
SPARKS: I'm sorry Captain.
GREY: I'm sure this can wait.
[''Something akin to a roar can be heard over THOMPSON's radio.'']
THOMPSON: It fucking broke through a building!
HAYES: Lucielle, are you all right?
THOMPSON: That damned orb is way closer than you said. There's debris everywhere and-
{{Blurimage|file=Shopping Cart in snow.jpg|caption=Still of light caused by the detonation, taken from HAYES's bodycam.}} FROST: THOMPSON! Calm down.
SPARKS: Lucy? Are you there?
THOMPSON: It's leaking!
GREY: What's leaking?
THOMPSON: The bomb, something cut-
[''An orange light flashes blinding the camera. SPARKS can be heard screaming before being muffled by a loud explosion. After a few seconds HAYES abruptly turns to FROST''.]
HAYES: I told you!
GREY: Hold on Sebastian.
FROST: Hayes how am I, in any way responsible for this?
HAYES: I told you the shell wasn't ready. You ignored me.
SPARKS: YOU KILLED HER!
[''The camera moves forward as it is believed SPARKS lunges at FROST who avoids him. The camera cracks as it impacts the ground.''] FROST: Be that as it may, I did not myself break it.
GREY: Guys... it's headed right towards us.
FROST: Get inside!
[''GREY immediately exits the tent and Hayes begins to run towards one of the buildings. A swooshing can be heard followed by several crashes, causing SPARKS and FROST to begin moving.''] GREY: Thanks for not killing Maizie, but you really shouldn't stand still with a living blizzard bearing down on you.
SPARKS: I haven't killed her ''yet''.
HAYES: Focus you two!
[''Hayes turns around and stops. A metal pole smacks into his head with a loud crack as he collapses.'']
GREY: Damn.
FROST: Fuck, we're not going to make it. Looks like you won't have a chance to kill me after all Sparks.
[''Frost turns around and brandishes her rifle, running past Sparks who briefly turns.'']
SPARKS: What are you doing now?
FROST: Buying you some time.
---- ===End Excerpt===
Operative Status
*Objective: Failed
*Lucielle Thompson and Sebastian Hayes have been deemed KIA.
* Maizee Frost has been deemed MIA.
Addendum 2:
'''Parties in Attendance''':
*Michael Sparks: Current overseer of '''Level 218''' liberation efforts. *Zachary Grey: [[Vouchsafed Contrivance|████]] supply master. * Ciel Parker: ████ operative. * Holly Gajus: Assigned [[M.E.G.]] researcher. *Nigel Baron: M.E.G. major armed forces member. '''Date''': November 6th, 2015 '''Location:''': [[Level 80]] near the exit to Base Bonfire. '''Source''': Audio recordings provided by Holly Gajus. ===Begin Excerpt === ---- GREY: Hold on Nigel, don't berate him.
BARON: The lives endangered by just letting a single entity take over a safe level cannot be ignored.
SPARKS: Class two-environmental. '''218''' wasn't safe.
GREY: I admit he does have a point. This is going to get people killed.
GAJUS: People have been killed either way. I don't like the solution but it's not the worst.
[''SPARKS can be heard slamming his hands on the table, triggering an audible gasp from PARKER''.] SPARKS: People have already been killed! And with that thing heading right towards Base Bonfire, I think it's safe to pull out now.
PARKER: And if Grey is right about it being some sort of weapon, the creators could send or make another one.
GREY: If it's a weapon, Holly's research would be very useful when it inevitably pops back up.
BARON: It could easily be useful now. But maybe I'm just glad to have an excuse to see something blow up.
GAJUS: I hardly think knowing it's magnetic can be considered 'useful'.
SPARKS: I lack your enthusiasm, Baron. The only thing we blew up last time was Lucielle.
GREY: Sir, that's barely relevant.
SPARKS: Exactly! It only shows we shouldn't blow stuff up carelessly.
[''A brief pause can be heard in the recording.'']
BARON: [''Whispering towards GAJUS.''] What the hell?
GAJUS: I have no idea either.
PARKER: Um... sir? If the metal is a shell, one bomb might not-
GREY: Can we maybe turn away from this topic?
SPARKS: No. This is important, regardless of my feelings. That doesn't mean pushing it, mind you.
NIGEL: So, the project stops today, what do you tell those afraid those who fall into '''218''' will die?
PARKER: Is that pushing it? I'll say it's a fair question.
SPARKS: There are a [[Entity List|million]] [[Level List|other]] [[Phenomenon List|ways]] to die in this hellhole. One more doesn't add much.
GAJUS: Wow... [''GAJUS pauses and chuckles uncomfortably''] That's harsh.
GREY: From their perspective, even one life lost is a considerable price.
BARON: I for one consider that an unsettling cost.
SPARKS: People are only afraid of being killed because they think they'll keep going for a while. Taking our future for granted.
GAJUS: Is that a comment on human bias or a continuation?
GREY: Give him his moment.
SPARKS: I've learned life doesn't care. You could die today, and the world would keep on spinning. Why should we waste our lives today for a future that we don't even know will happen?
PARKER: That's sad.
SPARKS: It's my honest thoughts on the matter.
GREY: And something Baron can tell his team to get them off your ass?
SPARKS: That too, you know me well Grey.
BARON: Well played.
PARKER: Still, those words mean something.
GAJUS: I'd rather not think about them, they'd make me a bit too introspective.
PARKER: That's a fair idea.
[''A shaking can be heard, likely coming from the door to '''Level 218'''.'']
GREY: Sounds like the thing's here. Can someone close the door?
BARON: Meeting's almost over. Why shouldn't we let some snow in?
SPARKS: I'll get it.
[''SPARKS's footsteps are audible. There's a crash and a shout from him.'']
GREY: Holy shit are you okay?
GAJUS: Was that a piece of drywall? Flew straight into his arm.
[''Three sets of footsteps run over. The door slams shut as SPARKS vocalizes in pain.'']
SPARKS: I think I'll need something medical.
=== End Excerpt ===
Notes
* Michael Sparks suffered a fractured arm with no additional injuries. He has made a full recovery. * Michael Sparks requested to leave ████ following this incident and has been transferred to the Iron Wall team. * Zachary Grey was put in charge of the project. * Nigel Baron applied for ████ and was later inducted into the team.
{{Notice|body=Information that is important but not part of this file version has been included below.}}
Addendum 3:
''The following text is a Backmail message sent to M.E.G. staff by Zachary Grey on December 24th, 2018.''
{{Blurquote|image=Shopping Cart in snow.jpg|quote-text=I believe it's finally time to move forward. While I'd hate to make Christmas a workday, it's not in M.E.G.'s nature to consider anything sacred. I think we're finally ready. Holly's research was a lifesaver, and Spark's policies did help to some degree. While [[Phenomenon 12|certain events]] did slow us down, we have still prospered. The plan is set up and awaiting execution. I don't know if any of us are going to make it out alive but let's keep our fingers crossed.|person=Merry Christmas!}}
Addendum 4:
Mission Summary:
*Captain: Zachary Grey *Vice-Captain: Ciel Parker *Technician: Holly Gajus *Operative(s): Nigel Baron *Objective: Terminate the Orb *Date: December 25th, 2018 *Source: Body camera footage from Zachary Grey, Radio logs == Begin Excerpt == ---- [''Footage opens to show GREY standing on the side of a building right next to a wire fence. Approximately 150 meters away, a different building has what appears to be a large metal disk and several speakers attached to it (Henceforth: The STRUCTURE). BARON is standing right in front of this structure holding an unknown object, dressed in heavy winter gear.'']
GREY: Are we all in position? I don't think we're in any position to back out now.
BARON: Back out? I've been waiting for this moment since you got promoted.
PARKER: And we can celebrate after, it's coming right at us.
GREY: Let him be enthusiastic. It could be his final chance to do so. I'm adding one more instruction.
BARON: Oh?
GREY: Don't fucking die.
GAJUS: If Nigel dies now, we won't have to hear him talk 24/7.
BARON: Yes, and you'll get to replace me as the team clown.
PARKER: Let's be safe.
GREY: Alright Nigel. It's in range, you're up.
BARON: Roger that.
[''The slightest bit of shaking, likely coming from The Orb can be seen on camera. Suddenly, the speakers on the STRUCTURE begin to loudly emit Mariah Carrey's All I Want for Christmas is You. PARKER can be heard lightly humming along.'']
GREY: That'll do the trick I guess...
PARKER: How far away is it?
GAJUS: About 300 meters from Baron... I'm not even going to question your music choices.
[''No speaking is heard over the radio for several seconds. The shaking intensifies and several flecks of snow can be seen in the distance coming from behind a building.'']
BARON: I've got a line of sight, seems like it's falling for the bait.
PARKER: Ready for cover fire.
GREY: And now, we wait. Do you have a backup track, Nigel?
====- 70 seconds of footage omitted -====
[''The Orb can be seen moving in front of the STRUCTURE as BARON breaks into a run to escape it.'']
BARON: Really cutting it close, are we?
GAJUS: Is it in position yet?
GREY: No, get it there Nigel... You can clip now.
PARKER: Launching the stun gun.
GREY: That's your cue.
[''The camera briefly flicks up to show PARKER standing on top of a distant building with a large weapon on the parapet, which emits a loud bang. The camera's view falls back down and shows the Orb which has ceased movement. BARON can be seen below it before he successfully noclips. Suddenly the silver disk on the STRUCTURE shakes before the Orb is pulled onto it with a loud crash.'']
GAJUS: Did we get it?
GREY: You got it.
[''A no-clip can be heard from the radio of GAJUS. The Camera rushes behind the wall of the nearby building before GREY crouches revealing a remote detonator.'']
GAJUS: We're out onto 306. It's even colder than 218.
PARKER: I've moved behind cover.
GREY: Are we ready for the finale?
PARKER: Punch it.
BARON: If you're gonna say a one-liner, now's the time.
GREY: Merry fucking Christmas.
[''GREY pressed the button and a loud explosion can be heard followed by a flash of light. GREY screams in triumph.'']
GREY: YES! IT'S DEAD! IT'S FINALLY FUCKING DEAD!
PARKER: Um... sir?
GREY: What? We've done it!
PARKER: Radio's out... and you should look around the corner.
[''The camera peeks around the building, revealing a white ball-shaped light rapidly approaching GREY.'']
{{Blurimage|file=The orb on a snowy day.jpg|caption=The last image of the Orb before it's destruction.|hovertext=Happy Holidays}} GREY: No... no... that's not fair. I can't die now.
PARKER: There's an iron pole here. It's broken...
[''GREY starts to run before collapsing to the ground, rolling over, and sobbing quietly. The camera shows the Orb turn the corner. After half a second it shatters and its light vanishes.'']
GREY: Ciel...
PARKER: You said don't die; I think that applies to you too.

---- == End Excerpt ==
